restrictions: Did not run build or lint because parent validator owns shared heavy checks

assertions.title: Event listeners use passive flag
assertions.reason: All identified scroll, resize, and touch listeners in the inspected code paths include `{ passive: true }`.

details: app-header.tsx and _profile+/_layout.tsx both register scroll listeners with `{ passive: true }`.
use-virtual-list.ts registers both resize and scroll listeners with `{ passive: true }`.
configure-fields-view.tsx and signature-pad/canvas.ts register resize listeners with `{ passive: true }`.
multiselect.tsx registers the touch listener with `{ passive: true }`.

assertions.title: Field components share a single resize listener
assertions.reason: Field and tooltip coordinate logic subscribes through `useSharedResize`, which centralizes resize handling behind one module-level listener.

details: use-shared-resize.ts maintains a module-level `Set` of callbacks plus an `isListening` flag.
The shared hook registers a single `window.addEventListener('resize', handleResize, { passive: true })` listener when the first subscriber mounts.
field-item.tsx, envelope-field-tooltip.tsx, envelope-recipient-field-tooltip.tsx, use-field-page-coords.ts, use-element-bounds.ts, and use-window-size.ts all call `useSharedResize(...)`.
No direct per-instance `window.addEventListener('resize')` calls remain in those consumer files.
The remaining direct resize listeners elsewhere are documented as intentionally separate in `.factory/library/client-side-data-fetching.md`.

summary: Validated 2 assertions via terminal inspection: VAL-CLIENT-001 passed because all identified scroll/resize/touch listeners use `{ passive: true }`, and VAL-CLIENT-003 passed because field and tooltip consumers share the centralized `useSharedResize` listener.
